name: pyproject-hooks

metadata.name: pyproject-hooks
metadata.summary: Wrappers to call pyproject.toml-based build backend hooks.

documentation:
# `pyproject-hooks`

This is a low-level library for calling build-backends in `pyproject.toml`-based project. It provides the basic functionality to help write tooling that generates distribution files from Python projects.

If you want a tool that builds Python packages, you'll want to use <https://github.com/pypa/build> instead. This is an underlying piece for `pip`, `build` and other "build frontends" use to call "build backends" within them.

You can read more in the [documentation](https://pyproject-hooks.readthedocs.io/).

> Note: The `pep517` project has been replaced by this project (low level) and the `build` project (high level).

modules.name: pyproject_hooks
documentation: Wrappers to call pyproject.toml-based build backend hooks.

variables.name: pyproject_hooks.BackendInvalid
documentation: Deprecated alias, previously a separate exception

modules.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process
documentation:
This is a subpackage because the directory is on sys.path for _in_process.py

The subpackage should stay as empty as possible to avoid shadowing modules that
the backend might import.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_proc_script_path
documentation: Python 3.8 compatibility

modules.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process
documentation:
This is invoked in a subprocess to call the build backend hooks.

It expects:
- Command line args: hook_name, control_dir
- Environment variables:
      _PYPROJECT_HOOKS_BUILD_BACKEND=entry.point:spec
      _PYPROJECT_HOOKS_BACKEND_PATH=paths (separated with os.pathsep)
- control_dir/input.json:
  - {"kwargs": {...}}

Results:
- control_dir/output.json
  - {"return_val": ...}

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.write_json
documentation: This file is run as a script, and `import wrappers` is not zip-safe, so we
include write_json() and read_json() from wrappers.py.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._build_backend
documentation: Find and load the build backend

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._supported_features
documentation:
Return the list of options features supported by the backend.

Returns a list of strings.
The only possible value is 'build_editable'.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.get_requires_for_build_wheel
documentation:
Invoke the optional get_requires_for_build_wheel hook

Returns [] if the hook is not defined.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.get_requires_for_build_editable
documentation:
Invoke the optional get_requires_for_build_editable hook

Returns [] if the hook is not defined.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel
documentation:
Invoke optional prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel

Implements a fallback by building a wheel if the hook isn't defined,
unless _allow_fallback is False in which case HookMissing is raised.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.prepare_metadata_for_build_editable
documentation:
Invoke optional prepare_metadata_for_build_editable

Implements a fallback by building an editable wheel if the hook isn't
defined, unless _allow_fallback is False in which case HookMissing is
raised.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._dist_info_files
documentation: Identify the .dist-info folder inside a wheel ZipFile.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._get_wheel_metadata_from_wheel
documentation:
Extract the metadata from a wheel.

Fallback for when the build backend does not
define the 'get_wheel_metadata' hook.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._find_already_built_wheel
documentation: Check for a wheel already built during the get_wheel_metadata hook.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.build_wheel
documentation:
Invoke the mandatory build_wheel hook.

If a wheel was already built in the
prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel fallback, this
will copy it rather than rebuilding the wheel.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.build_editable
documentation:
Invoke the optional build_editable hook.

If a wheel was already built in the
prepare_metadata_for_build_editable fallback, this
will copy it rather than rebuilding the wheel.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.get_requires_for_build_sdist
documentation:
Invoke the optional get_requires_for_build_wheel hook

Returns [] if the hook is not defined.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.build_sdist
documentation: Invoke the mandatory build_sdist hook.

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.BackendUnavailable
documentation: Raised if we cannot import the backend

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.HookMissing
documentation: Raised if a hook is missing and we are not executing the fallback

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._BackendPathFinder
documentation:
Implements the MetaPathFinder interface to locate modules in `backend-path`.

Since the environment provided by the frontend can contain all sorts of
MetaPathFinders, the only way to ensure the backend is loaded from the
right place is to prepend our own.

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process._DummyException
documentation: Nothing should ever raise this exception

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._in_process._in_process.GotUnsupportedOperation
documentation: For internal use when backend raises UnsupportedOperation

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.default_subprocess_runner
documentation:
The default method of calling the wrapper subprocess.

This uses {func}`subprocess.check_call` under the hood.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.quiet_subprocess_runner
documentation:
Call the subprocess while suppressing output.

This uses {func}`subprocess.check_output` under the hood.

functions.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.norm_and_check
documentation:
Normalise and check a backend path.

Ensure that the requested backend path is specified as a relative path,
and resolves to a location under the given source tree.

Return an absolute version of the requested path.

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.SubprocessRunner
documentation: A protocol for the subprocess runner.

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BackendUnavailable
documentation: Will be raised if the backend cannot be imported in the hook process.

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.HookMissing
documentation: Will be raised on missing hooks (if a fallback can't be used).

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.UnsupportedOperation
documentation: May be raised by build_sdist if the backend indicates that it can't.

classes.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.__init__
documentation:
---
param backend_path: Additional path entries for the build backend spec
param build_backend: The build backend spec
param python_executable: The Python executable used to invoke the build backend
param runner: The {ref}`subprocess runner <Subprocess Runners>` to use
param source_dir: The source directory to invoke the build backend for
---

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.subprocess_runner
documentation:
A context manager for temporarily overriding the default
{ref}`subprocess runner <Subprocess Runners>`.

```
param runner

The new subprocess runner to use within the context.
```

```python
hook_caller = BuildBackendHookCaller(...)
with hook_caller.subprocess_runner(quiet_subprocess_runner):
    ...
```

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller._supported_features
documentation: Return the list of optional features supported by the backend.

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.get_requires_for_build_wheel
documentation:
Get additional dependencies required for building a wheel.

```
param config_settings

The configuration settings for the build backend

returns

A list of .
```

:::{admonition} Fallback
If the build backend does not defined a hook with this name, an
empty list will be returned.
:::

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel
documentation:
Prepare a `*.dist-info` folder with metadata for this project.

```
param metadata_directory

The directory to write the metadata to

param config_settings

The configuration settings for the build backend

param _allow_fallback

Whether to allow the fallback to building a wheel and extracting
the metadata from it. Should be passed as a keyword argument only.

returns

Name of the newly created subfolder within
metadata_directory, containing the metadata.
```

:::{admonition} Fallback
If the build backend does not define a hook with this name and
`_allow_fallback` is truthy, the backend will be asked to build a
wheel via the `build_wheel` hook and the dist-info extracted from
that will be returned.
:::

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.build_wheel
documentation:
Build a wheel from this project.

```
param wheel_directory

The directory to write the wheel to

param config_settings

The configuration settings for the build backend

param metadata_directory

The directory to reuse existing metadata from

returns

The name of the newly created wheel within wheel_directory.
```

:::{admonition} Interaction with fallback
If the `build_wheel` hook was called in the fallback for
{meth}`prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel`, the build backend would
not be invoked. Instead, the previously built wheel will be copied
to `wheel_directory` and the name of that file will be returned.
:::

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.get_requires_for_build_editable
documentation:
Get additional dependencies required for building an editable wheel.

```
param config_settings

The configuration settings for the build backend

returns

A list of .
```

:::{admonition} Fallback
If the build backend does not defined a hook with this name, an
empty list will be returned.
:::

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.prepare_metadata_for_build_editable
documentation:
Prepare a `*.dist-info` folder with metadata for this project.

```
param metadata_directory

The directory to write the metadata to

param config_settings

The configuration settings for the build backend

param _allow_fallback

Whether to allow the fallback to building a wheel and extracting
the metadata from it. Should be passed as a keyword argument only.

returns

Name of the newly created subfolder within
metadata_directory, containing the metadata.
```

:::{admonition} Fallback
If the build backend does not define a hook with this name and
`_allow_fallback` is truthy, the backend will be asked to build a
wheel via the `build_editable` hook and the dist-info
extracted from that will be returned.
:::

methods.name: pyproject_hooks._impl.BuildBackendHookCaller.build_editable
documentation:
Build an editable wheel from this project.

```
param wheel_directory

The directory to write the wheel to

param config_settings

The configuration settings for the build backend

param metadata_directory

The directory to reuse existing metadata from

returns

The name of the newly created wheel within wheel_directory.
```

:::{admonition} Interaction with fallback
If the `build_editable` hook was called in the fallback for
{meth}`prepare_metadata_for_build_editable`, the build backend
would not be invoked. Instead, the previously built wheel will be
copied to `wheel_directory` and the name of that file will be
returned.
:::
